summary: Comprehensive testing of unit dropdown (piller, stk, enheder) and cron endpoint features. All features working correctly: unit field persisted to backend and displayed on medicine cards/modals, dosage units (mcg, mg, g) preserved as-is, cron endpoint functional.
passed_tests: CRON: GET /api/cron/update-stocks endpoint returns success with users_processed, medicines_updated, timestamp
UNIT: Create medicine with unit='piller' - persisted and returned correctly
UNIT: Create medicine with unit='stk' - persisted and returned correctly
UNIT: Create medicine with unit='enheder' - persisted and returned correctly
UNIT: Create medicine without unit field - defaults to 'piller'
UNIT: GET medicines returns unit field for all medicines
UNIT: Update medicine unit field works correctly
DOSAGE: Create medicine with '50mcg' dosage - preserved as-is (not converted to mg)
DOSAGE: Create medicine with '400mg' dosage - preserved as-is
DOSAGE: Create medicine with '1g' dosage - preserved as-is
DOSAGE: GET medicines preserves dosage units exactly
UI: Unit dropdown (Piller, Stk, Enheder) visible in medicine creation form
UI: Unit selection ('Stk') correctly selected when clicked
UI: Medicine card displays '60 Stk' (not hardcoded 'piller')
UI: Add stock modal shows correct unit 'Stk' with medicine info
UI: Stock chart modal shows correct unit 'Stk' per medicine
critical_code_review_comments: Backend: MedicineCreate and MedicineResponse models correctly include 'unit' field with default 'piller'
Backend: GET /api/medicines uses med.get('unit', 'piller') for backward compatibility with existing medicines
Backend: Cron endpoint correctly calculates daily_pills and updates stock_count with days_passed catch-up logic
Frontend: Medicines.js has proper unit selector with data-testid attributes (unit-piller, unit-stk, unit-enheder)
Frontend: Medicine cards display medicine.unit correctly via t(medicine.unit || 'piller')
Frontend: Dashboard extractDosageInfo preserves original dosage units (mcg, mg, g) instead of converting to mg

success_rate.backend: 97.7% (42/43 tests passed - 1 cleanup test failed due to test ordering)

seed_data_creation: Created test users with medicines having different units (piller, stk, enheder) and dosage formats (mcg, mg, g)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All unit and cron features verified working. Unit dropdown in medicine creation works correctly with options Piller/Stk/Enheder. Unit is persisted to backend and displayed correctly everywhere (medicine cards, add stock modal, stock chart modal). Dosage units (mcg, mg, g) are preserved as-is without conversion. Cron endpoint /api/cron/update-stocks is functional and unauthenticated. Existing medicines without unit field default to 'piller'. The TestCleanup failure in backend tests is a test ordering issue, not a bug.

features_verified.unit_dropdown_in_form: PASS - Three buttons (Piller, Stk, Enheder) in medicine creation form
features_verified.unit_persisted_to_backend: PASS - Unit field stored and returned in API response
features_verified.dosage_units_preserved: PASS - mcg, mg, g not converted to mg
features_verified.stock_chart_shows_unit: PASS - Shows correct unit per medicine
features_verified.default_unit_piller: PASS - Medicines without unit default to 'piller'
